name: Peach Slump
recipeIngredient: 8 cups thinly sliced peeled peaches (approx 8 medium size peaches)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Lightly butter a 1 1/2-quart casserole dish.
3. In a medium-sized bowl, toss together the peaches, brown sugar, almond extract, and cinnamon. Spread in the bottom of the casserole dish.
4. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and 2 tablespoons of white sugar. Cut the butter into the flour mixture until it has the consistency of coarse crumbs.
5. Combine the milk, buttermilk, and egg with the flour mixture. Do not knead; the dough will be sticky, stiff, and lumpy.
6. Drop by spoonfuls over the peaches. Try to get even coverage, but don't worry about a few bare spots.
7. Bake for about 25 minutes, until the top is golden and the juices are bubbling.
8. Cool on a wire rack for about 5 minutes. Then loosen the biscuit from the sides of the pan and invert onto a serving platter.
